9//
10// This file declares the Argument class.
11//
12//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
13
14#ifndef LLVM_IR_ARGUMENT_H
15#define LLVM_IR_ARGUMENT_H
16
17#include "llvm/ADT/Twine.h"
18#include "llvm/ADT/ilist_node.h"
19#include "llvm/IR/Attributes.h"
20#include "llvm/IR/Value.h"
21
22namespace llvm {
23
24template<typename ValueSubClass, typename ItemParentClass>
25 class SymbolTableListTraits;
26
27/// \brief LLVM Argument representation
28///
29/// This class represents an incoming formal argument to a Function. A formal
30/// argument, since it is ``formal'', does not contain an actual value but
31/// instead represents the type, argument number, and attributes of an argument
32/// for a specific function. When used in the body of said function, the
33/// argument of course represents the value of the actual argument that the
34/// function was called with.
35class Argument : public Value, public ilist_node<Argument> {
36 virtual void anchor();
37 Function *Parent;
38
39 friend class SymbolTableListTraits<Argument, Function>;
40 void setParent(Function *parent);
41
42public:
43 /// \brief Constructor.
44 ///
45 /// If \p F is specified, the argument is inserted at the end of the argument
46 /// list for \p F.
47 explicit Argument(Type *Ty, const Twine &Name = "", Function *F = nullptr);
48
49 inline const Function *getParent() const { return Parent; }
50 inline Function *getParent() { return Parent; }
51
52 /// \brief Return the index of this formal argument in its containing
53 /// function.
54 ///
55 /// For example in "void foo(int a, float b)" a is 0 and b is 1.
56 unsigned getArgNo() const;
57
58 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the nonnull attribute on it in
59 /// its containing function. Also returns true if at least one byte is known
60 /// to be dereferenceable and the pointer is in addrspace(0).
61 bool hasNonNullAttr() const;
62
63 /// \brief If this argument has the dereferenceable attribute on it in its
64 /// containing function, return the number of bytes known to be
65 /// dereferenceable. Otherwise, zero is returned.
66 uint64_t getDereferenceableBytes() const;
67
68 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the byval attribute on it in its
69 /// containing function.
70 bool hasByValAttr() const;
71
72 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the byval attribute or inalloca
73 /// attribute on it in its containing function. These attributes both
74 /// represent arguments being passed by value.
75 bool hasByValOrInAllocaAttr() const;
76
77 /// \brief If this is a byval or inalloca argument, return its alignment.
78 unsigned getParamAlignment() const;
79
80 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the nest attribute on it in its
81 /// containing function.
82 bool hasNestAttr() const;
83
84 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the noalias attribute on it in its
85 /// containing function.
86 bool hasNoAliasAttr() const;
87
88 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the nocapture attribute on it in
89 /// its containing function.
90 bool hasNoCaptureAttr() const;
91
92 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the sret attribute on it in its
93 /// containing function.
94 bool hasStructRetAttr() const;
95
96 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the returned attribute on it in
97 /// its containing function.
98 bool hasReturnedAttr() const;
99
100 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the readonly or readnone attribute
101 /// on it in its containing function.
102 bool onlyReadsMemory() const;
103
104 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the inalloca attribute on it in
105 /// its containing function.
106 bool hasInAllocaAttr() const;
107
108 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the zext attribute on it in its
109 /// containing function.
110 bool hasZExtAttr() const;
111
112 /// \brief Return true if this argument has the sext attribute on it in its
113 /// containing function.
114 bool hasSExtAttr() const;
115
116 /// \brief Add a Attribute to an argument.
117 void addAttr(AttributeSet AS);
118
119 /// \brief Remove a Attribute from an argument.
120 void removeAttr(AttributeSet AS);
121
122 /// \brief Method for support type inquiry through isa, cast, and
123 /// dyn_cast.
124 static inline bool classof(const Value *V) {
125 return V->getValueID() == ArgumentVal;
126 }
127};
128
129} // End llvm namespace
130
131#endif
